summary: Hotz announces that AMD is sending tinygrad two MI300X boxes and explains why he's betting big on AMD stock. He argues that CUDA's moat is overstated since tinygrad has built a fully sovereign AMD stack, and that AMD's 16x lower valuation compared to NVIDIA doesn't make sense given similar hardware capabilities. He's put $250,000 into AMD stock as a long-term bet that AI demand is just beginning and good software will let AMD hardware outperform NVIDIA.
key_points: AMD passed Hotz's 'cultural test' by not sabotaging themselves on software, which he sees as their historical weakness
CUDA isn't a real moat - it's just an early ecosystem advantage that can be replicated
tinygrad has built a complete sovereign AMD stack from hardware to PyTorch (except LLVM)
NVIDIA artificially halves tensor core throughput on consumer cards while AMD doesn't on RDNA4
Either NVIDIA is overvalued or AMD is undervalued - the 16x valuation gap doesn't reflect hardware reality
money_quotes: AMD passed my cultural test. I now believe they aren't going to shoot themselves in the foot on software.
CUDA isn't really the moat people think it is, it is just an early ecosystem.
tiny corp has a fully sovereign AMD stack, meaning we have rewritten the full stack from the hardware to PyTorch.
Either NVIDIA is super overvalued or AMD is undervalued.
If the petaflop gets commoditized (tiny corp's mission), the current situation doesn't make any sense.
NVIDIA artificially halves this on their cards, soon market pressure will force them not to.
I'm betting on AMD being undervalued, and that the demand for AI has barely started.
Bought in for a quarter million. Long term.
key_insight: NVIDIA's dominance rests on software ecosystem lock-in rather than hardware superiority, and tinygrad's sovereign AMD stack proves that moat can be crossed.
